A  few examples: A  number of abbreviations are commonly used in text messages, especially by 4 txt   msgs    (text   messages) Tel     01466  790256 Yorkshire              LS7 3P0 Carstairs 63 Baker      Lane Robin Yours New address etc below.

are times when they talk a language I don't understand. © Enjoying the village - very interesting world this, but there Also signed up for a creative writing course - don’ t laugh!

I am working like crazy - still a lot to do around the house.

How  about 29 or 30  Sept?

Good to hear from you.

Subject:       new address Cc...

ɪɑ     •               phil@gcom.org .net Example      of  an   informal      email / 'forward slash’   \ ‘ backslash’   : ‘ colon’ Note also the names of symbols in 'urls' (internet addresses): the_rabbit@coolmail.gr ‘ the underscore rabbit at coolmail dot g r' emily@log-farm.com         ‘ emily at log dash farm dot com ’ j.harris@funbiz.co.uk        'j dot harris at funbiz dot co dot u k' email addresses are read as follows: 3 addresses grammar •  291   abbreviated styles Love, Amy                                                                         14.15 Hope to see you.

visits to cheer me up.

If you have time please tell the others - would love some doctor for cold - landed up in hospital with pneumonia!!

for me. Yes, you've guessed - in hospital!! Only went to No washing-up. Clean sheets every day. Everything done Watching tennis on TV. A  good  book. Three meals a day.

The  same style is common in postcards, emails and text messages.

see Josh 11.00 meeting Emma  lunch Gone to hairdresser. Back 12.30. Get tickets phone Anna Informal notes, to-do lists, diary entries, etc often follow similar rules.

sugar and leave on low heat for 45 minutes.

Pour mixture into large saucepan, heat until boiling then add three pounds Job needed urgently. Will do anything legal. Call 312654.

Single man looking for flat Oxford area. Phone 806127 weekends.

forms of be and prepositions.

Small ads and instructions often leave out articles, subject or object pronouns, 1  advertisements           and   instructions because of the need to save space or time.

Some styles of writing and speech have their own special grammar rules, often 291 abbreviated         styles tx, thx thanks ruok? Are you OK?                                      tbh to be honest omg   Oh,   my  God!                                 rn  right now nm   never mind                                np  no problem lol   laugh out loud                            ngl  not gonna lie jk just kidding                                l8r  later Written Texts   Section 26 grammar •  291   abbreviated styles For other rules about leaving words out ('ellipsis'), ►    275-280.

RECORD    DRUGS    HAUL   AT  AIRPORT:   SIX  HELD  FOUR   DIE IN M6  BLAZE For details, ►    292.

News headlines have their own special grammar and vocabulary.
